title: The best 'print-on-demand' services for books

author.name: Marcus Thorne
author.bio: A former tech COO turned 'Business-of-One' consultant, Marcus is obsessed with efficiency. He writes about optimizing workflows, leveraging technology, and building resilient systems for solo entrepreneurs.

key_takeaways: Purchase your own ISBN to act as the publisher of record, ensuring full control and distribution flexibility across all platforms.
For non-US professionals, submit a W-8BEN form to each US-based platform to prevent a default 30% withholding tax on American royalties.
Implement a hybrid distribution strategy by using KDP for Amazon-only sales and IngramSpark for global reach, making sure to decline KDP's 'Expanded Distribution' option.
Establish a dedicated, multi-currency bank account to receive all royalty payments, which simplifies financial reconciliation and minimizes currency conversion fees.
summary: To transform your book into a true business asset, you must first avoid the common pitfall of ceding control through "free" platform services like ISBNs. The core advice is to secure your intellectual property by purchasing your own ISBN and registering your copyright, then implement a hybrid distribution strategy using both KDP for Amazon's ecosystem and IngramSpark for global reach. This professional approach ensures you retain full ownership, maximize market saturation, and can fully integrate your book as a powerful, profitable marketing engine for your core business.
ai_overview_answer: To turn a book into a business asset using print-on-demand, a professional must first secure the intellectual property and then implement a hybrid distribution strategy for maximum control. The most critical step is purchasing your own ISBN to become the publisher of record, which grants you the flexibility to use any printer or distributor. You must also formally register your copyright to protect your work and, if you are a non-US professional, file a W-8BEN form with US platforms to avoid a 30% royalty withholding tax. The optimal distribution model combines Amazon KDP for its marketplace dominance with IngramSpark for its global reach to over 40,000 retailers and libraries. To execute this strategy, you must decline KDP's "Expanded Distribution" option, allowing IngramSpark to handle all non-Amazon channels without conflict. Manage your multi-platform revenue professionally by using a dedicated bank account for all royalties and performing monthly financial reconciliations. Finally, leverage the book as a marketing asset to generate leads, establish authority, and create high-ticket service offerings.

step.name: Securing Your Asset
step.text: Purchase your own ISBNs through the official agency in your country to list yourself as the publisher, giving you ultimate flexibility. Formally register your manuscript with your country's copyright office to create a public record of ownership and enable legal action against infringement. For non-US professionals, complete and submit a W-8BEN form to each US-based platform to prevent a default 30% withholding tax on royalties from US sales.

step.name: The Hybrid Distribution Engine
step.text: Implement a hybrid strategy by using Amazon KDP for sales within its ecosystem and IngramSpark for global distribution to over 40,000 retailers and libraries. This is only possible if you own your ISBN. When setting up on KDP, you must decline the 'Expanded Distribution' option to prevent channel conflict. Use specialty platforms like Lulu for high-margin direct sales or Blurb for premium, visually-focused projects.

step.name: Integrating Revenue into Your Business
step.text: Create a dedicated bank account, preferably multi-currency, to receive all royalty payments. Centralize this income in your accounting software with specific categories for each platform. Conduct a monthly financial reconciliation by matching platform sales reports to bank deposits. Minimize fee erosion from currency conversions by using services like Wise or Revolut. Finally, leverage the book as a marketing asset to generate leads for high-ticket services, establish credibility, and create new business offers.

mainEntity.name: Can you really use both KDP and IngramSpark for the same book?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. This hybrid strategy is the professional standard for achieving total market saturation. It requires that you own your ISBN. The key is to configure the channels correctly: use KDP for Amazon's ecosystem but decline their "Expanded Distribution" option. This prevents channel conflicts and allows IngramSpark to handle distribution to the rest of the world, including bookstores and libraries that won't order from Amazon.

mainEntity.name: What are the key tax implications for a non-US professional?

acceptedAnswer.text: The primary risk is a mandatory 30% withholding tax on royalties from US sources, like Amazon.com. To prevent this, you must submit a Form W-8BEN to each US-based platform. This form certifies your foreign status and allows you to claim tax treaty benefits, which often reduce the withholding to 0%. Remember, you must still declare all royalty income in your home country according to local tax laws.

mainEntity.name: Which platform offers more control: KDP or IngramSpark?

acceptedAnswer.text: They offer different *types* of control. IngramSpark provides superior *publisher-level control*, positioning you as the publisher of record for the global retail and library network. KDP offers unrivaled *marketplace control* within the Amazon ecosystem. The CEO mindset doesn't choose between them; it integrates both, leveraging each for its core strength.

mainEntity.name: Is buying my own ISBN truly necessary?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, it is a non-negotiable investment for any serious professional. A "free" ISBN makes the platform the publisher of record, permanently locking your book to their service. Purchasing your own ISBN makes *you* the publisher, granting you the essential flexibility to use multiple printers, engage different distribution channels, and control your brand's identity across the entire market.

mainEntity.name: How does print quality compare across platforms?

acceptedAnswer.text: While quality is consistent across major platforms, IngramSpark and Lulu are often regarded as having more premium paper and hardcover options. KDP's quality is reliable and perfectly suitable for most professional paperbacks. However, the only way to ensure the final product meets your brand standards is to follow one unbreakable rule: always order a proof copy from every service you intend to use. This is a fundamental step in risk management.

body.content: The promise of transforming your book into a powerful business asset begins not with creative choices, but with foundational business decisions. Before you analyze paper weights or cover finishes, a CEO secures the asset itself. This critical first phase addresses the compliance and intellectual property issues that most self-publishing guides gloss over, giving you the peace of mind to build your book-based business on solid ground.
Phase 1: Securing Your Asset
The ISBN Decision: Why "Free" Is the Most Expensive Option

The International Standard Book Number (ISBN) is a 13-digit code that uniquely identifies your book globally. Platforms like KDP Print and Lulu will offer you a "free" one, but this convenience comes at a steep price: control.

When you accept a free ISBN, the platform becomes the official **publisher of record**. This locks your specific book format into their ecosystem forever. If you want to move to another printer or use a hybrid distribution strategy later, you will be forced to get a new ISBN, effectively creating a completely separate product in the marketplace. For a business owner, this is an unacceptable risk.

Purchasing your own ISBNs through the official agency in your country (like Bowker in the United States) is the single most important investment you can make. It lists *you* or your company as the publisher, giving you the ultimate flexibility to print, distribute, and control your book across any platform, now and in the future. This is the foundational step for acting as a serious publisher, not just an author.
Securing Your Intellectual Property

Your manuscript is more than just content; it is valuable **intellectual property** (IP). While copyright is technically granted the moment you create the work, formally registering it with your country's official copyright office is a non-negotiable step for any professional.

This registration creates a public record of your ownership and is a prerequisite for taking legal action to defend your work against infringement. In many jurisdictions, such as the US, you cannot even file an infringement lawsuit without it. Think of it as the official deed to your property—an essential risk-mitigation tactic that provides the legal backstop you need to protect your asset.
The W-8BEN Mandate: Protecting Your US Royalties

For the global professional operating outside the United States, this is the single biggest—and most avoidable—financial threat. US law requires platforms like Amazon to withhold a default 30% of any royalties earned from US sales for tax purposes. For many, that's a catastrophic loss of revenue.

The solution is the **W-8BEN** form, the "Certificate of Foreign Status of Beneficial Owner." By completing this form for *each* US-based platform you use, you certify your non-US status and claim benefits under the tax treaty between your country and the US. For professionals in many countries, this can reduce the withholding tax from 30% to 0%. Failing to handle this simple piece of compliance is like setting fire to a third of your US income. A CEO would never allow it, and neither should you.
Phase 2: The Hybrid Distribution Engine

With your asset secured and your finances protected, it's time to shift from defense to offense. A CEO doesn't just choose a single vendor; they design a resilient system engineered for total market saturation. This means rejecting the false choice between platforms and instead building a powerful hybrid distribution engine to maximize both your reach and your control.
The Core Strategy: KDP for Dominance, IngramSpark for Reach
The definitive professional strategy involves leveraging the two largest players in the print-on-demand space for their distinct strengths. This is only possible because you own your ISBN.

items: **Amazon KDP for Maximum Amazon Visibility:** Use KDP Print to directly service the world's largest bookstore. This provides two key advantages: potentially higher royalties on Amazon sales and the best possible positioning within its massive ecosystem.

**IngramSpark for Global Reach:** Simultaneously, use IngramSpark to push the *exact same book* (using the same ISBN) into its global distribution network. This network is vast, reaching over 40,000 retailers, libraries, universities, and independent bookstores that often refuse to order from Amazon, their main competitor.

body.content: **Crucial Configuration:** To prevent channel conflict, this step is non-negotiable. When you set up your book in KDP, you must **decline** the "Expanded Distribution" option. This tells Amazon to sell your book only on their own properties. IngramSpark will then handle distribution to everyone else, creating a perfectly complementary system.
A CEO's Platform Analysis: The Control Matrix

Forget generic feature lists. A business owner analyzes platforms based on the levers of control they provide. This matrix evaluates the top players through the lens of what truly matters for your business.
Specialty Platforms for Strategic Goals
While KDP and IngramSpark form your core distribution engine, specialty platforms like Lulu and Blurb are powerful tools for specific, high-margin objectives.

Think of them not as your primary channel, but as strategic assets. Use **Lulu** for high-quality direct sales from your own website, leveraging its Shopify integration to capture nearly all of the revenue from your most loyal followers. Use **Blurb** when visual quality is paramount—for art books, portfolios, or premium corporate gifts. Their reputation is built on superior print quality for visually-driven projects, allowing you to create high-value products for targeted goals outside the mass market.
Phase 3: Integrating Revenue into Your Business

Executing strategic sales is one thing; managing the complex, multi-platform revenue that follows is where the real work of a CEO begins. Your book's revenue isn't a side hustle; it's an integrated part of your business's cash flow. This final phase addresses the financial mechanics that ensure your new asset strengthens, not complicates, your core business.
The Multi-Platform Reconciliation Plan

Once your hybrid distribution engine is running, you will receive small, irregular payments from multiple platforms. They will arrive on different schedules, in different currencies, and with different fee structures. This is an accounting nightmare waiting to happen. As Manasa Nadig, an Enrolled Agent and Founder of MN Tax & Business Services, notes, "When you have multiple streams of income from different platforms and currencies, a clear financial system is non-negotiable. Without it, you're not just risking inaccurate tax filings; you're operating blind. You can't make strategic decisions because you don't have a clear picture of your actual profitability."
To maintain control, you need a simple, repeatable system:

items: **Create a Dedicated Bank Account.** Do not mix your book revenue with your primary business income. Open a separate account (ideally one that handles multiple currencies well) to receive all royalty payments. This creates a clean ledger.

**Centralize in Your Accounting Software.** Connect this new bank account to your platform of choice, such as Xero or QuickBooks. Create specific income categories for each POD platform (e.g., "Royalty Income: KDP," "Royalty Income: IngramSpark").

**Conduct a Monthly Financial Reconciliation.** At the end of each month, download the sales reports from each platform. Match the deposits in your dedicated bank account to these reports. This is the only way to verify you've been paid correctly and to maintain a true, real-time view of your book's financial performance.

body.content: Minimizing "Fee Erosion"

The price of the book is not the final word on your profit. A significant portion of your earnings can be lost to "fee erosion"—a series of small cuts that add up to a substantial loss if left unmanaged.

items: **Currency Conversion Fees:** When a platform like Amazon.com pays out US dollars to your Euro or Pound Sterling account, your bank will take a significant margin on the conversion. **Strategy:** Use a multi-currency business account like Wise or Revolut to receive payments in their native currency and convert the funds at a much lower, more transparent rate.

**Payment Thresholds & Schedules:** Most platforms have payment thresholds (e.g., you won't be paid until you've earned $100) and fixed payment schedules (e.g., 60 days after the end of the month in which the sale occurred). You must factor this delay into your cash flow projections. This isn't immediate income; it's income you must plan for.

body.content: Leveraging Your Book as a Marketing Asset
The book's true return on investment often lies beyond royalties. For a professional, the book is not the end product; it is a powerful marketing engine for your core, high-ticket services.

items: **The High-Ticket Lead Magnet:** Offer a physical copy of your book to highly qualified leads. The perceived value is exponentially higher than a PDF and immediately establishes your authority.

**The Credibility Keystone:** Sending a professionally published book to a potential client or speaking engagement organizer before a meeting fundamentally changes the dynamic. You are no longer just a consultant; you are a published expert.

**The Foundation for New Offers:** Use the book's content as the basis for a paid workshop, a corporate training program, or a high-value consulting package. The book validates the offer and serves as the curriculum, allowing you to scale your expertise.

body.content: Frequently Asked Questions
Navigating these strategic decisions inevitably brings up practical questions. Here, we address the most common queries with the direct, actionable clarity you need.

items:
**Can you really use both KDP and IngramSpark for the same book?**
Yes. This hybrid strategy is the professional standard for achieving total market saturation. It requires that you own your ISBN. The key is to configure the channels correctly: use KDP for Amazon's ecosystem but **decline their "Expanded Distribution"** option. This prevents channel conflicts and allows IngramSpark to handle distribution to the rest of the world, including bookstores and libraries that won't order from Amazon.

**What are the key tax implications for a non-US professional?**
The primary risk is a mandatory 30% withholding tax on royalties from US sources, like Amazon.com. To prevent this, you must submit a **Form W-8BEN** to each US-based platform. This form certifies your foreign status and allows you to claim tax treaty benefits, which often reduce the withholding to 0%. Remember, you must still declare all royalty income in your home country according to local tax laws.

**Which platform offers more control: KDP or IngramSpark?**
They offer different *types* of control. **IngramSpark** provides superior *publisher-level control*, positioning you as the publisher of record for the global retail and library network. **KDP** offers unrivaled *marketplace control* within the Amazon ecosystem. The CEO mindset doesn't choose between them; it integrates both, leveraging each for its core strength.

**Is buying my own ISBN truly necessary?**
Yes, it is a non-negotiable investment for any serious professional. A "free" ISBN makes the platform the publisher of record, permanently locking your book to their service. Purchasing your own ISBN makes *you* the publisher, granting you the essential flexibility to use multiple printers, engage different distribution channels, and control your brand's identity across the entire market.

**How does print quality compare across platforms?**
While quality is consistent across major platforms, IngramSpark and Lulu are often regarded as having more premium paper and hardcover options. KDP's quality is reliable and perfectly suitable for most professional paperbacks. However, the only way to ensure the final product meets your brand standards is to follow one unbreakable rule: **always order a proof copy** from every service you intend to use. This is a fundamental step in risk management.

body.content: Your Book Is an Asset. Manage It Like One.

Stepping back from the tactical details, a larger strategic picture emerges. The conversation shifts from picking a printing service to architecting a business. You are no longer just an author; you are the CEO of a media company, and this book is your flagship asset. This change in mindset is the single most powerful tool you have.

First, you **secure the asset.** A CEO doesn’t build on a faulty foundation. This means rejecting "free" ISBNs that surrender control, formally registering your intellectual property, and proactively managing compliance risks like the W-8BEN form. These aren't administrative chores; they are fundamental acts of asset protection.

With the asset secured, you **build the distribution engine.** You move beyond the simplistic "either/or" debate of platforms. A CEO builds a system optimized for total market saturation, leveraging KDP for its dominance within Amazon and IngramSpark for its unmatched global retail and library network. This hybrid approach is the definitive strategy for professionals who demand both deep market penetration and complete control.

Finally, you **integrate the finances.** A book's revenue is not pocket money; it's a vital cash flow stream for your business. This requires a professional approach: a dedicated bank account, meticulous reconciliation, and a clear-eyed strategy to minimize fee erosion. This disciplined framework allows you to manage for profitability and understand your asset's true return on investment. By following this playbook, you move from simply publishing a book to strategically launching a valuable, scalable, and fully integrated business asset.
